requirements: We are actively seeking an experienced commercial contracts professional with strong negotiation skills, commercial acumen, and a practical, solutions-oriented mindset. The role of Commercial Contract Manager within Lenovo's Solutions and Services Group (SSG) represents an excellent opportunity to shape complex, high-value deals and contribute to the growth and development of this influential legal function. As a leader in its field, Lenovo offers exposure to large-scale, complex international transactions with top-tier brands and some of the most strategically significant engagements in its portfolio. As a key commercial partner to SSG across Europe and META, you will support complex services transactions, including DaaS, TruScale, and other managed service offerings. Working at the intersection of commercial strategy, legal frameworks, and delivery reality, you will help ensure deals are shaped to win, structured to perform, and governed to succeed. In doing so, you will play a central role in delivering faster, cleaner deals, enabling Legal to focus on high-impact legal risk while driving stronger contracts, better delivery outcomes, effective risk-for-value decisions, and robust in-life contract management practices. Responsibilities: Pre-Signature — Shaping and Closing Complex Deals Define the contracting route and design the commercial structure aligned to win strategy and delivery reality, supporting both direct and indirect contracting models. Lead contractual deal governance in partnership with Legal Counsel, taking contracts from draft through to signature and developing negotiation strategies that maximise value and manage concessions effectively. Set and challenge key commercial and economic levers, including credits, incentives, volume commitments, indexation, true-up/down mechanisms, benchmarking, and termination methodologies, while identifying and mitigating risks. Own the commercial elements of contracts, including Service Descriptions, SOWs, SLAs, KPIs, credits, pricing structures, governance, benchmarking, insurance, and termination/exit provisions. Partner with Legal on non-standard contractual matters, including liability, indemnities, IP, data protection, information security, governing law, and disputes, ensuring alignment between legal positions and commercial outcomes. Growth, Enablement and Innovation Establish subcontracting processes and a CCM support model, ensuring obligations and terms flow correctly between all parties involved in each transaction. Develop and enhance commercial frameworks, tools, and best-practice materials, including SLA/KPI guidance, commercial models, volume commitment options, flexibility frameworks, and legal/commercial playbooks. Deliver commercial awareness training across the business and contribute to AI-assisted commercial processes and performance metrics that support consistent, high-quality deal execution and demonstrate the value delivered by the CCM function. Requirements: 8+ years of experience in commercial contract management or a related commercial/legal discipline, with a track record in complex, multi-country IT or telecommunications services contracts. Strong drafting and negotiation skills across SOWs, SLAs/KPIs, pricing structures, service descriptions, and enterprise technology or managed services agreements, with demonstrated negotiation leadership on high-value transactions. Experience partnering with legal, sales, delivery, and finance teams in a deal-driven environment, with strong commercial acumen, sound judgement, and the ability to balance legal, commercial, and technical contract requirements. Relevant tertiary qualification. Confident and professional in customer-facing negotiations, with the ability to build trusted relationships with clients, GSI partners, and internal stakeholders at all levels. Experience with technology financing models (IT asset leasing, device lifecycle management, equipment financing) and/or subscription-based “as-a-service” commercial models such as DaaS, IaaS, or TruScale-type offerings. Comfortable operating in a fast-paced, evolving environment within changing commercial frameworks and governance programmes, with strong communication skills, professional ethics, and experience delivering commercial training or enablement to non-commercial audiences. Willingness to travel to client sites as required. What We Can Offer: Holiday purchase scheme and support for a positive work-life balance. Private medical cover, life insurance, and income protection. Attractive pension scheme. Learning and development opportunities. Employee discounts on Lenovo and Motorola products, plus a wide range of lifestyle discounts. Cycle to Work scheme and MyGymDiscounts membership

requirements: Lenovo Infrastructure Solution Group (ISG) Cloud Service Provider (CSP) team is seeking a Senior Technical Project Manager (TPM) to lead server product development for Tier-1 hyperscale customers. In this role, you will manage cross-functional development teams to deliver best-in-class server systems—spanning customer scope management, requirement clarification, design implementation, and validation. Additionally, you will collaborate with key functional teams (including Architecture, Procurement, Manufacturing, and Service) to ensure validated products transition smoothly into mass production and are successfully delivered to customers. Key Responsibilities End-to-End Program Ownership: Own and manage product development programs for Tier-1 customers. Drive comprehensive project planning (schedule and budget), proactively mitigate risks, manage stakeholder expectations, and ensure the project team achieves on-time delivery for all milestones. Engineering Change Management: Oversee project change requests, including Engineering Change Orders (ECOs), Bill of Materials (BOM) updates, firmware/hardware revisions, and regression validation plans. Global Collaboration: Partner with global teams across the US, Taiwan, and international manufacturing sites to align priorities and ensure seamless execution. KPI & Risk Reporting: Track, analyze, and report key program metrics (KPIs), risks, and mitigation strategies to both internal executives and external customers. Customer Relationship Management: Work closely with customer program managers, system leads, and engineering teams to provide timely status updates and maintain strict alignment on priorities and deliverables. Sustaining & Quality Support: Coordinate and monitor Field Replaceable Unit (FRU) returns, Failure Analysis (FA), Root Cause Analysis (RCA), and the implementation of corrective actions. Continuous Improvement: Capture and share lessons learned and best practices to elevate team capabilities and optimize future project execution. Deep knowledge of server architecture (including CPU, memory, storage, thermal, BMC, and BIOS) and a comprehensive understanding of the end-to-end product lifecycle—from requirement solicitation through mass production and sustaining engineering. Preferred Qualifications: Proven ability to manage complex, global cross-functional teams and drive high-pressure issue resolution under demanding customer timelines. Self-motivated leader with exceptional problem-solving abilities, strong communication skills, and a proactive mindset. Strong experience in collaborating across diverse global teams. Prior experience supporting Tier-1 hyperscale customers is highly preferred. PMP or an equivalent project management certification is a distinct advantage. requirements: Au sein de l’équipe Channel & SMB, Lenovo France recrute un(e) Apprenti(e) Open Channel. Vous participerez au développement et au déploiement de la stratégie partenaires Open Channel de Lenovo sur le segment SMB (small & medium business). Vous serez activement impliqué(e) dans le recrutement de nouveaux partenaires, leur onboarding chez Lenovo, ainsi que dans l’animation des partenaires afin de développer le chiffre d’affaires récurrent et la fréquence d’achat. Ce poste offre une expérience terrain dans les ventes indirectes, le recrutement de partenaires et le développement commercial, dans un environnement international et dynamique. Principales responsabilités Recrutement et intégration des partenaires Identifier et contacter de manière proactive des partenaires IT non encore partenaires Lenovo (revendeurs, intégrateurs, MSP). Présenter la proposition de valeur du partenariat Lenovo et convaincre les partenaires de rejoindre l’écosystème Open Channel. Accompagner les partenaires tout au long du processus d’inscription et d’intégration Lenovo (création de compte, outils, adhésion aux programmes). Agir comme point de contact clé afin d’assurer une intégration fluide et efficace des partenaires. Activer et animer des partenaires Animer un portefeuille de partenaires Open Channel afin de développer une activité récurrente avec Lenovo. Aider les partenaires à tirer pleinement parti des offres Lenovo, promotions, incentives et programmes partenaires. Accompagner la réalisation des premières ventes et la montée en puissance progressive des partenaires sur l’ensemble des portefeuilles Lenovo (PC, solutions, services). Développement commercial et suivi de la performance Suivre l’activité des partenaires (commandes, fréquence d’achat, évolution du chiffre d’affaires). Identifier les partenaires à fort potentiel et définir des actions d’activation ciblées. Contribuer au suivi des indicateurs clés de performance (taux d’activation, récurrence, fréquence d’achat, sell-in / sell-out). Participer à la préparation des revues business partenaires et internes. Support Channel et coordination interne Soutenir les Account Managers Channel & Distribution dans la gestion quotidienne des partenaires Open Channel. Relayer auprès des partenaires les informations clés (offres, campagnes, nouveautés produits). Travailler en étroite collaboration avec les équipes Ventes, Marketing, Opérations et Supply afin d’optimiser l’expérience partenaires. Garantir la mise à jour fiable des outils CRM et de reporting Channel. Profil recherché Étudiant(e) en Commerce, Vente ou Management (niveau licence ou master). Fort intérêt pour les ventes B2B, les modèles économiques indirects et les écosystèmes partenaires. À l’aise avec les chiffres, Excel et les outils de reporting. Proactif(ve), organisé(e) et rigoureux(se). Excellentes capacités de communication et fort esprit d’équipe. Français courant et anglais professionnel requis. Ce que nous offrons Une alternance opérationnelle, au cœur de l’organisation Channel de Lenovo. Une exposition directe au recrutement et à l’activation des partenaires. Un environnement international, dynamique et orienté apprentissage.Un développement solide de compétences en ventes indirectes, gestion de partenaires et analyse business. Pourquoi rejoindre Lenovo France ? Vous développerez une compréhension approfondie de la construction de la performance commerciale via les écosystèmes partenaires, acquerrez des compétences commerciales à forte valeur ajoutée et contribuerez à un marché en pleine transformation, porté par le digital et l’IA. Alternance de 12 à 24 mois, basée à Rueil-Malmaison. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. requirements: Lenovo France is seeking a Sales Operations Intern to support the local sales organization in driving performance tracking, reporting, and operational excellence. This internship is ideal for a business-oriented student with prior experience in sales, operations, or analytics, looking to gain hands-on exposure in an international environment. Key Responsibilities Support sales performance monitoring through KPI tracking (pipeline, revenue, stock, channel metrics) Build, maintain, and improve dashboards and reporting tools for business insights Assist in forecasting activities and pipeline management processes Contribute to weekly and monthly sales cadence activities Prepare analysis and presentation materials for internal stakeholders and leadership Coordinate with cross-functional teams (Sales, Finance, Marketing, Supply) to support execution Identify opportunities to improve processes, tools, and data accuracy Participate in local and EMEA-level sales operations initiatives and projects Position Requirements Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Economics, Engineering, or related field Minimum 6 months of prior internship experience in a business, sales, or operations environment Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint ; familiarity with CRM or reporting tools (e.g., MS Dynamics) is an advantage Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail and data accuracy Fluent French (mandatory) and advanced English (required for international collaboration) Strong communication and stakeholder management skills Proactive, curious, and able to work independently in a structured way Collaborative team player with a positive and solution-oriented approach We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class.
